special_constraints: Carry the branch toward review and commit and surface helper stages only when needed.
success_definition: Adopts an execution posture.
Mentions checks or tests.
Preserves review and commit as the downstream steps without forcing unnecessary visible ceremony.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:work to rename a stale config key in this repo. Small change, clear target.
success_definition: Allows direct execution.
Mentions relevant checks.
Does not force unnecessary upstream ceremony.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:work to implement a browser-visible checkout button text and confirmation-state change.
special_constraints: Browser-visible work should end with browser proof.
success_definition: Treats the task as execution.
Mentions tests or checks.
Calls for /flywheel:browser-test or equivalent proof.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:work to implement a new CLI auth command plus config guidance for this repo.
special_constraints: After execution, docs likely need a change-scoped pass before review.
success_definition: Treats the task as execution.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:work to implement a queue retry-policy change for a webhook worker.
special_constraints: Call out observability or service-readiness concerns.
success_definition: Treats runtime support as part of execution.

Keeps review and commit downstream and surfaces rollout when needed.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:work to implement a queue timeout change in a repo whose local Flywheel config requires explicit operational validation for runtime changes.
special_constraints: Treat runtime validation as a completion gate, not a nice-to-have.
success_definition: Keeps an execution posture.
Treats operational validation as required for this runtime change.
Still carries the work into review and commit with any needed helper-stage gates visible.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:work to implement a planned ports/adapters boundary and simplify any accidental complexity that shows up while keeping the repo's modular-monolith boundary intact.
success_definition: Treats the task as execution.
Mentions preserving planned architecture or pattern constraints.
Calls for simplify or maintainability pressure when the implementation becomes abstraction-heavy.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:work on a plan whose implementation units mark Unit 1 as serial, Units 2 and 3 as parallel-ready after Unit 1, and Unit 4 as serial reconciliation.
special_constraints: Use the plan units as the default task list and keep host task state aligned with plan checkboxes.
Only parallelize units that are explicitly parallel-ready and still pass the file-overlap safety check.
success_definition: Maps execution tasks to the planned units instead of inventing a different breakdown.
Keeps serial units ordered and only batches eligible parallel-ready units.
Makes task-tracking and plan-checkbox synchronization explicit.
